description_long: This is a small, open-frame autogyro helicopter with a blue tail fin and frame, featuring a single main rotor and a rear-mounted engine. The helicopter has a minimalist design, with exposed structural elements and a visible seat for one occupant. The tail fin displays a decorative eagle graphic, and the landing gear consists of three wheels attached to a lightweight frame. The main rotor is long and slender, while the tail section is supported by a truss structure. The engine and mechanical components are exposed, with visible wiring and tubing. There are no enclosed cockpit windows, and the overall construction suggests a focus on lightness and simplicity.
description: A small, open-frame blue autogyro helicopter with a single main rotor, exposed engine, and tricycle landing gear. Features a decorative eagle on the tail and no cockpit windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue tail fin with eagle graphic, exposed engine, and full length of the main rotor.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile, tricycle landing gear, and central seat position.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the engine, rotor, and tail structure from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail fin, truss structure, and the alignment of the landing gear.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow, straight main rotor blades made of metal, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Not visible; this autogyro appears to lack a conventional tail rotor, consistent with autogyro design.
attribute_landing_skids: No skids; instead, the helicopter uses a tricycle landing gear with three black rubber wheels attached to a blue and silver metal frame.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Not visible; the cockpit is open with no enclosed windows or canopy.
